domain: Hopi katsina spirit-mask ritual (Pueblo Southwest; dancer's personal identity ritually suppressed when wearing mask; spirit-being's persona overlays; many distinct masks for 500+ katsinim; reversible upon mask removal; schedule-locked ritual window)
specific_mechanism: Identity-null-space substitution via ritual mask — dancer's everyday persona suppressed while katsina persona is overlaid; mask is a CONDUIT, not container; swap of mask = swap of persona; reversal restores everyday self.
llm_application: KATSINA-MASK: a runtime persona-overlay mechanism with (a) precomputed PER-PERSONA PROJECTION MATRIX M_k ∈ R^(d×d) learned from calibration data; (b) inference-time decomposition of hidden states h_ℓ = M_k h_ℓ + (I − M_k) h_ℓ at selected layers; (c) NULL-SPACE RESIDUAL (I − M_k) h_ℓ suppressed by factor β ∈ [0,1] (β=0 full mask, β=1 no mask); (d) mask MATRIX SWAP at runtime without retraining; (e) FULLY REVERSIBLE — removing the mask restores original h_ℓ. Used for safety-persona switching (child-safe / expert-mode), tone control, role-play boundaries.
novelty_claim: Persona prompts (system message) steer via tokens. ICLR 2026 persona-specialized subnetworks (2602.07164) IDENTIFY existing subnetworks via masking calibration but do NOT define a runtime projection-and-swap mechanism. Persona Non Grata (2604.11120) is an evaluation benchmark. KATSINA-MASK differs by (a) ACTIVE RUNTIME PROJECTION (not just identification), (b) PER-PERSONA M_k SWAPPABLE, (c) NULL-SPACE RESIDUAL SUPPRESSION coefficient β, (d) EXPLICIT REVERSIBILITY, (e) HIDDEN-STATE LEVEL (not prompt-token level).
